meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop waiting for inspiration. Learn how to leverage portfolio building to generate SaaS ideas, validate revenue models, and ship faster using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing 'the perfect idea.' Instead, build a portfolio of micro-SaaS tools that solve specific, high-friction problems for niche audiences. By shipping small, modular projects using AI-augmented workflows, you generate real-world data, build authority, and pivot toward high-revenue opportunities based on actual user demand rather than theoretical speculation.
content.intro:
The blank page is the graveyard of ambition. You stare at the screen, paralyzed by the fear that your next project won't be a 'unicorn,' so you build nothing at all. This perfectionism is a slow-motion failure that keeps you from the only thing that actually matters: market feedback.

Portfolio building is your antidote. It isn't about padding a resume; it's about rapid prototyping to uncover what people will actually pay for. By treating your development cycle as a series of experiments rather than a quest for the 'one big idea,' you transform from a stagnant dreamer into a high-velocity founder.

steps.title: Define Your Niche Problem Space
steps.description: Identify a specific workflow bottleneck in a boring industry. We aren't looking for innovation; we're looking for inefficiency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Analyze current pain points in the 'freelance accounting' sector. Identify 3 micro-SaaS opportunities that solve specific, high-frequency manual tasks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'research niche accounting bottlenecks and output a JSON list of 3 MVP feature sets'

steps.title: Bootstrap the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold a lean, production-ready stack. Focus on core functionality only.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 project with Tailwind CSS and Supabase integration. Include a simple user auth flow and a dashboard view.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate project boilerplate with auth and database schema'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your primary business logic, ensuring clean separation of concerns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for a tool that automates invoice parsing using the OpenAI API. Ensure secure error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'implement invoice parsing service with OpenAI SDK'

steps.title: Build for Monetization
steps.description: Don't hide your pricing. Integrate Stripe early to validate the 'willingness to pay' metric.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe checkout integration for a monthly subscription model. Include a middleware check for user subscription status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'integrate Stripe billing and protect routes based on subscription status'

steps.title: Deploy and Iterate
steps.description: Push to production. Your goal is to get one user, not one thousand. Feedback starts the moment you ship.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment guide for Vercel and a post-launch checklist for tracking user conversion metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate deployment script and monitoring setup for Vercel'

pro_tips: Always build the payment gate before the feature; if no one clicks, you saved yourself weeks of wasted dev time.
Use 'Feature Flagging' to hide incomplete modules, allowing you to deploy daily without breaking the user experience.
Focus on 'Boring SaaS'—tools that solve existing, expensive problems are 10x easier to sell than novel, unproven inventions.
Treat your codebase as disposable; if a project doesn't get traction in 30 days, kill it and reuse the components for the next iteration.
Build a 'public build' page to share your progress; founders who build in public attract partners and early adopters faster.
content.next_steps: After shipping your first MVP, spend 80% of your time on distribution and 20% on features. If you don't have 10 users in 14 days, kill the project and iterate on the next one.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect moment to start your journey. The most successful founders are the ones who build, fail, and iterate faster than the competition. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
